com.testingtech.tworkbench.ttman.server.api

Interface IExecutionHandler

  • All Known Subinterfaces:
    ICampaignExecutionHandler
    All Known Implementing Classes:
    DefaultHandler, ProxyHandler

    public interface IExecutionHandler
    Interface for asynchronous feedback from the TTman server to the client. The server calls this interface methods when a control part or a test case was started or has ended or when the server has been shut down. Input requests from the TTCN-3 Test Environment are passed to the teRequest(ITERequest) method, see ITEResponse for details on how to respond to these requests.
    Since:
    TTworkbench 15

    • Method Summary

      All Methods Instance Methods Abstract Methods 
      Modifier and Type Method and Description
      ITEResponse teRequest(ITERequest request)
      Callback for SUT actions for showing messages and built-in external functions like timedMessageBox and messageBox inside the TTlibrary module TTlibrary_UI for requests awaiting a input from the management side.

    • Method Detail

      • teRequest

        ITEResponse teRequest(ITERequest request)
               throws java.io.IOException
        Callback for SUT actions for showing messages and built-in external functions like timedMessageBox and messageBox inside the TTlibrary module TTlibrary_UI for requests awaiting a input from the management side.
        Parameters:
        request - a request object
        Returns:
        a response object that will be serialized using ITEResponse.getEncodedResult() and ITEResponse.getEncoding()
        Throws:
        java.io.IOException - is thrown on communication error 
        public ITEResponse teRequest(ITERequest request) throws IOException {
    String message = null;
    switch (request.getKind()) {
      // here you can handle the automation of build in TTCN-3 statement
      // "action" (aka SUTaction)
      case message: {
        message = ((TEMessageRequest) request).getMessage();
        // Please add here your test automation code!
        System.out.println(MessageFormat.format("Question asked by test execution: \"{0}\"", message));
        return new TEResponse(StatusImpl.OK, null, null);

      }

      // here you can handle the automation of the external function
      // "messagebox" as been provided by TTlibrary_UI!
      case selection: {
        String description = ((TESelectionRequest) request).getDescription();
        String title = ((TESelectionRequest) request).getTitle();
        String[] labels = ((TESelectionRequest) request).getButtonLabels();

        int i = 0;
        for (String string : labels) {
          // Please add here your test automation code!
          // For example you could parse the information and depending on
          // the content of
          // * description
          // * title
          // * button labels, etc

          System.out.println(MessageFormat.format("Label#:  \"{0}\" \n The lable is: \"{1}\"", i++, string));

        }
        System.out.println(MessageFormat.format("Window title: \"{0}\"", title));

        int integer = (int) (labels.length * 1.0 * Math.random());
        IntegerTEResponse integerTEResponse = new IntegerTEResponse(integer);
        return integerTEResponse;
      }
    }
    throw new IOException("Action not implemented in TTmanExampleClient");
}
